results.title: 13 cantos of hell: images from the divine comedy of Dante
results.creator_credit: Experimental Committee of the British Film Institute
results.brief_description: An animated film based on episodes from Dante’s Inferno. The film is the work of the young artist Peter King, who was formerly associated with Lotte Reininger, famous for her silhouette films. A similar technique is used in this film in which the animated figures have a sculptural form. They display a highly individual visual imagination complemented by compelling sound.

results.title: 01 = Zero one. [Ep. 73/10]
results.brief_description: Cinemagazine items including: 1. Arlesondt, situated on one of Norway’s countless fjiords - fishing agriculture and factory work. 2. First ever World Helicopter Championships (12 nations) take place on an airfield near Hanover in the north of Germany. 3. New field of application for plastic polypropylene in the manufacture of car batteries. 4. Blood banks can save many people’s lives. 5. Water from a river on the Franco-German border considered as the dirtiest river in Europe is to test a new purifying agent.

results.title: 01 = Zero one. [Ep. 73/05]
results.brief_description: Cinemagazine items including: 1. International Air Show in Paris, 1971. 2. Deep silos - used for conserving feed for livestock. 3. New method to prevent cars “aqua-planing” being tried out in the building of motorways and other fast roads in Europe. 4. New centre for the treatment of diseases of the urinary system opened near Cologne. 5. Tuning, hotting-up, sooping-up - increasing the performance of a normal car - how its done.

results.title: [Wings to Vikingland]
results.brief_description: A tour of Sweden, Norway and Denmark by an American couple. The receive a traditional Swedish welcome in Stockholm; visit Lapland and the ancient city of Visby; Norway with its many fiords and mountains; the Viking and Ski Mountains; and finally Denmark - their tour of this country included visits to the royal castle, parliament building and University of Copenhagen.
